description_long: This object is a vintage rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust rectangular black base with a circular rotary dial on its front face. The dial is marked with white numbers and a central label reading 'BELL SYSTEM,' surrounded by a metallic ring. The handset, finished in black with bronze metallic end caps, rests on a cradle supported by two metal hooks. A thick, black cord connects the handset to the left side of the base, looping in a gentle arc. The base is solid and slightly stepped, with a metallic lever on the right side, likely for switching the line. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, combining metal and plastic elements for a classic, durable appearance.
description: A vintage black and bronze rotary dial corded telephone with a solid rectangular base, metallic accents, and a classic handset connected by a thick cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset resting on the cradle, with the cord emerging from the left and the metallic lever visible on the right.
description_view_1: Direct front view highlighting the rotary dial, central label, and the full face of the base with both handset ends visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the handset and the cord attachment, with the metallic lever on the left side.
description_view_3: Back view showing the rear of the base, the full arc of the cord, and the handset resting on the cradle.
